categories.name: Automotive Electronics
categories.description: BYD Automotive Electronics include vehicle control units, battery management systems, motor controllers, and sensors for new energy vehicles and traditional automotive applications.
categories.longDescription: BYD's automotive electronics portfolio leverages the company's extensive experience as a leading NEV manufacturer. The product range includes high-performance vehicle control units (VCUs) that manage powertrain operations, advanced battery management systems (BMS) that ensure safe and efficient battery operation, and precision motor controllers for electric drive systems. All products are AEC-Q100/Q101 qualified and designed to meet the stringent reliability requirements of automotive applications. With vertical integration from semiconductor to system level, BYD provides comprehensive automotive electronics solutions. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive Automotive Electronics selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

series.name: Vehicle Control Units
series.description: High-performance controllers for vehicle powertrain management

series.name: Battery Management Systems
series.description: Comprehensive BMS solutions for lithium battery packs

selectionGuide.title: How to Select BYD Automotive Electronics?
selectionGuide.description: Choose the right automotive electronics based on vehicle type, voltage platform, and functional requirements

faqs.question: What automotive qualifications do BYD electronics products have?
faqs.answer: BYD automotive electronics products carry comprehensive automotive qualifications: (1) AEC-Q100 for integrated circuits and AEC-Q101 for discrete semiconductors. (2) ISO 26262 functional safety certification for safety-critical components. (3) IATF 16949 quality management system certification for manufacturing. (4) EMC compliance per CISPR 25 and ISO 11452. (5) Environmental testing per IEC 60068 including temperature, humidity, vibration, and mechanical shock. These qualifications ensure reliable operation in the demanding automotive environment with 15-year design life.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for automotive qualification documents and PPAP support.

faqs.question: What voltage platforms does BYD automotive electronics support?
faqs.answer: BYD automotive electronics support multiple voltage platforms: (1) 12V/48V systems for mild hybrid and traditional vehicles. (2) 400V platform for mainstream EVs including passenger cars and commercial vehicles. (3) 800V platform for high-performance EVs enabling faster charging and higher efficiency. (4) Custom voltage solutions for special applications. The product portfolio includes level shifters, isolation devices, and voltage regulators to support mixed-voltage architectures. BYD's experience as an EV manufacturer ensures products are optimized for real-world automotive voltage conditions including transients and load dumps.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for voltage platform selection and architecture design.

faqs.question: How does BYD ensure functional safety in automotive electronics?
faqs.answer: BYD ensures functional safety through comprehensive design processes: (1) ISO 26262 compliant development process from concept to production. (2) ASIL decomposition and safety analysis for systems up to ASIL D. (3) Redundant architectures with diagnostic coverage >99% for critical functions. (4) Built-in self-test (BIST) capabilities for continuous monitoring. (5) Fail-safe modes that place the system in a safe state upon fault detection. (6) Comprehensive FMEA and FTA during design phase. BYD's VCU products include dual-core lockstep CPUs, ECC memory protection, and extensive diagnostic functions. Safety manuals and FMEDA documents are available for system-level safety analysis.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for functional safety documentation and system design support.

products.shortDescription: High-quality automotive electronics component designed for reliable performance in industrial and commercial applicat...
products.description: BYD-VCU-400V is a high-performance vehicle control unit designed for 400V electric vehicle platforms. It integrates motor control, battery management communication, and vehicle networking functions with ASIL-C functional safety.
descriptionParagraphs: 400V Vehicle Control Unit for EV powertrain management. Dual-core ARM Cortex-R52 with lockstep operation.
IP67 enclosure with automotive-grade connectors. Operating temperature -40°C to +85°C.
In stock for EV development. FAE support for integration and calibration.
features: Support for 400V EV platforms up to 300kW

faqs.question: What processors are used in BYD-VCU-400V?
faqs.answer: BYD-VCU-400V uses dual-core ARM Cortex-R52 processors running at 300MHz with lockstep operation for safety. The lockstep configuration compares outputs of both cores cycle-by-cycle to detect faults. Additional features include: (1) ECC protected memory for data integrity. (2) Memory protection unit (MPU) for task isolation. (3) Hardware floating-point unit for control algorithms. (4) Dual watchdog timers for fault detection. This architecture provides ASIL-C safety level suitable for powertrain control applications. The processor subsystem is qualified to AEC-Q100 Grade 1.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for processor performance analysis and software development support.

faqs.question: What communication interfaces does BYD-VCU-400V support?
faqs.answer: BYD-VCU-400V provides comprehensive communication interfaces: (1) CAN-FD: 4 channels with up to 5Mbps data rate for vehicle networking. (2) Ethernet: 100BASE-T1 automotive Ethernet for high-speed data. (3) LIN: 2 channels for low-cost sensor networks. (4) FlexRay: Optional for high-speed deterministic communication. (5) Digital I/O: 32 channels for sensor/actuator interface. (6) Analog inputs: 16 channels with 12-bit ADC. All interfaces include ESD protection and EMC filtering. The VCU supports AUTOSAR communication stacks and J1939 protocol for commercial vehicles.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for communication architecture design and protocol support.

faqs.question: What safety features are included in BYD-VCU-400V?
faqs.answer: BYD-VCU-400V includes comprehensive safety features: (1) Dual-core lockstep CPU with comparator for fault detection. (2) ECC on all memories (Flash, RAM, cache) for data integrity. (3) Clock monitoring with backup oscillator for fail-safe operation. (4) Voltage monitoring for power supply faults. (5) Temperature monitoring with derating. (6) Watchdog timers with independent clock source. (7) Safe state entry upon fault detection. (8) Diagnostic coverage >99% for single-point faults. These features enable ASIL-C certification per ISO 26262. Safety manual provides detailed safety analysis for system integration.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for safety analysis and system-level safety design.

faqs.question: What is the power consumption of BYD-VCU-400V?
faqs.answer: BYD-VCU-400V power consumption: (1) Typical operation: 15W at 12V input with all interfaces active. (2) Low-power mode: 5W in standby with wake-on-CAN capability. (3) Peak power: 25W during startup and flash programming. (4) Power supply: 9-16V DC input with reverse polarity protection. (5) Power management: Configurable sleep modes for vehicle stationary operation. The VCU includes internal DC-DC converters with >90% efficiency. Power consumption monitoring is available for energy management. Thermal design should account for 25W peak dissipation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for power budget analysis and thermal design.

faqs.question: What software support is available for BYD-VCU-400V?
faqs.answer: BYD provides comprehensive software support for VCU-400V: (1) AUTOSAR Classic Platform 4.4 with MCAL drivers. (2) RTOS: AUTOSAR OS or FreeRTOS options. (3) Communication stacks: CANopen, J1939, UDS diagnostic. (4) Bootloader: UDS-based flash programming. (5) Calibration: XCP/CCP protocol support. (6) Development tools: Compiler, debugger, and configuration tools. (7) Sample applications: Motor control, BMS interface, vehicle state management. (8) Technical documentation: User manual, API reference, integration guide. Software licenses are available for production use. BYD also provides training for software development.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for software licensing and development support.

faqs.question: How do I integrate BYD-VCU-400V with battery management systems?
faqs.answer: Integrating BYD-VCU-400V with BMS: (1) Communication: Use CAN-FD for high-speed BMS data exchange. Standard protocol supports up to 16 battery modules. (2) Data exchange: Cell voltages, temperatures, SOC, SOH, fault status. (3) Safety: Redundant safety path via discrete I/O for emergency disconnect. (4) Power: VCU provides wake-up signal to BMS for coordinated sleep/wake. (5) Diagnostics: UDS diagnostic services for BMS fault reading. (6) Calibration: XCP protocol for BMS parameter calibration. BYD provides BMS interface specification and sample integration code. The VCU is tested with BYD's BMS products and compatible with standard CAN-based BMS.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for BMS integration support and protocol details.

faeReview.author: Senior FAE

products.shortDescription: 16-cell battery management system for lithium battery packs with active balancing
products.description: BYD-BMS-16S is a 16-cell battery management system for lithium-ion battery packs. It provides cell monitoring, active balancing, temperature monitoring, and safety protection for energy storage and EV applications.
descriptionParagraphs: 16-cell BMS with active balancing for lithium battery packs. Supports LiFePO4 and NMC chemistries.
High-precision cell voltage measurement with 1mV accuracy. CAN communication for system integration.
In stock for battery pack development. FAE support for BMS configuration and integration.
features: Active balancing up to 200mA per cell

faqs.question: What battery chemistries does BYD-BMS-16S support?
faqs.answer: BYD-BMS-16S supports multiple lithium battery chemistries: (1) LiFePO4 (Lithium Iron Phosphate): 3.2V nominal, 2.5-3.6V operating range. (2) NMC (Nickel Manganese Cobalt): 3.7V nominal, 3.0-4.2V operating range. (3) NCA (Nickel Cobalt Aluminum): 3.6V nominal, 3.0-4.2V operating range. (4) LTO (Lithium Titanate): 2.4V nominal, 1.8-2.8V operating range. Chemistry selection is configurable via software. The BMS automatically adjusts voltage thresholds and balancing parameters based on selected chemistry. Contact BYD for support of other chemistries.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for chemistry selection and BMS configuration.

faqs.question: How does the active balancing work in BYD-BMS-16S?
faqs.answer: BYD-BMS-16S active balancing system: (1) Method: Energy transfer from high cells to low cells using inductor-based bidirectional DC-DC converters. (2) Current: Up to 200mA balancing current per cell. (3) Efficiency: >85% energy transfer efficiency. (4) Control: Automatic balancing when cell voltage difference exceeds threshold (configurable, default 50mV). (5) Speed: Typical 1-2 hours to balance pack with 10% SOC imbalance. (6) Benefits: Higher efficiency than passive balancing, longer battery life, better pack performance. Active balancing is particularly beneficial for large packs and systems with frequent cycling.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for balancing system design and optimization.

faqs.question: What safety protections does BYD-BMS-16S provide?
faqs.answer: BYD-BMS-16S comprehensive safety protections: (1) Cell overvoltage: Disconnects charger when any cell exceeds threshold. (2) Cell undervoltage: Disconnects load when any cell falls below threshold. (3) Overcurrent: Fast protection for charge and discharge overcurrent. (4) Overtemperature: Reduces current or disconnects at high temperature. (5) Short circuit: Hardware protection with fast response (<100μs). (6) Leakage detection: Insulation monitoring for high-voltage systems. (7) Precharge: Controls precharge for safe connection to capacitive loads. All protections have configurable thresholds and hysteresis. Faults are logged with timestamp for diagnostics.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for safety configuration and protection settings.

faqs.question: What is the measurement accuracy of BYD-BMS-16S?
faqs.answer: BYD-BMS-16S measurement accuracy specifications: (1) Cell voltage: ±1mV accuracy at 25°C, ±3mV over -20°C to +60°C. (2) Pack current: ±0.5% of full scale with Hall sensor interface. (3) Temperature: ±1°C accuracy with 10k NTC thermistors. (4) SOC estimation: ±3% accuracy with Kalman filter algorithm. (5) SOH estimation: ±5% accuracy based on impedance tracking. High accuracy enables precise battery management, maximizing usable capacity and detecting cell degradation early. Calibration is performed at factory; no field calibration required.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for measurement accuracy analysis and calibration.

faqs.question: How do I configure BYD-BMS-16S for my application?
faqs.answer: Configuring BYD-BMS-16S: (1) Software tool: Windows-based configuration software via CAN interface. (2) Parameters: Cell chemistry, voltage thresholds, temperature limits, balancing settings, CAN IDs. (3) Calibration: Factory calibrated; user calibration for current sensor offset. (4) Firmware update: CAN-based firmware update capability. (5) Configuration storage: Parameters stored in internal Flash. (6) Default config: Generic LiFePO4 settings for quick startup. BYD provides configuration templates for common applications. Configuration file can be saved and loaded for production programming. Password protection prevents unauthorized changes.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for configuration support and application templates.

faqs.question: Can multiple BYD-BMS-16S modules be stacked for larger packs?
faqs.answer: Yes, BYD-BMS-16S supports stacking for larger battery packs: (1) Master-slave architecture: Up to 16 slave modules controlled by one master. (2) Communication: CAN bus between modules with isolated interface. (3) Synchronization: Cell voltage sampling synchronized across all modules. (4) Balancing: Coordinated balancing across entire pack. (5) Contactors: Master controls main contactors based on all module status. (6) Addressing: Rotary switch or software addressing for module ID. Configuration supports up to 256 cells (16 modules × 16 cells). Stacking enables scalable solutions from small packs to large energy storage systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for multi-module system design and integration.

categories.name: Power Semiconductors
categories.description: BYD Power Semiconductors include IGBT modules, power MOSFETs, SiC devices, and rectifier modules for energy conversion, motor control, and power management applications.
categories.longDescription: BYD's power semiconductor portfolio leverages the company's extensive experience in new energy vehicles and energy storage systems. The product range includes high-performance IGBT modules for industrial drives and EVs, advanced SiC MOSFETs for high-frequency applications, and power MOSFETs for switching power supplies. All products are manufactured using advanced packaging technology with excellent thermal performance and reliability. BYD's vertical integration from chip design to module packaging ensures high quality and cost-effectiveness. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive Power Semiconductors selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

series.name: IGBT Modules
series.description: High-power IGBT modules for industrial and automotive applications

series.name: SiC MOSFETs
series.description: Silicon carbide devices for high-frequency power conversion

selectionGuide.title: How to Select BYD Power Semiconductors?
selectionGuide.description: Choose the right power semiconductor based on voltage, current, switching frequency, and efficiency requirements

faqs.question: What voltage ratings are available for BYD IGBT modules?
faqs.answer: BYD IGBT modules are available in voltage ratings from 600V to 1200V: (1) 600V-650V series for industrial motor drives and 400V EV systems. (2) 750V series for commercial vehicle applications. (3) 1200V series for 800V EV platforms, solar inverters, and industrial drives. The voltage rating should be selected with adequate margin above the DC bus voltage. BYD provides detailed voltage derating curves for reliable operation under various conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for voltage rating selection based on your application requirements.

faqs.question: What are the advantages of BYD SiC MOSFETs over IGBTs?
faqs.answer: BYD SiC MOSFETs offer significant advantages over IGBTs: (1) Higher switching frequency - up to 100kHz vs 20kHz for IGBT, enabling smaller magnetics. (2) Lower switching losses - reduced energy loss during switching transitions. (3) Lower conduction losses at high temperatures due to positive temperature coefficient. (4) No tail current - faster turn-off without current tail. (5) Higher efficiency - typically 1-3% improvement in system efficiency. (6) Higher temperature operation - up to 175°C junction temperature. These advantages make SiC ideal for high-frequency, high-efficiency applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for SiC vs IGBT comparison and application recommendations.

faqs.question: What packages are available for BYD power semiconductors?
faqs.answer: BYD power semiconductors are available in various packages: (1) Discrete packages - TO-220, TO-247, D2PAK for MOSFETs and diodes. (2) Module packages - 34mm, 62mm, EasyPACK for IGBT modules. (3) Advanced packages - TOLL, DFN for high-density applications. (4) Custom packages - available for high-volume applications. Package selection depends on power level, thermal requirements, and mounting constraints. All packages use advanced materials for optimal thermal performance and reliability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for package selection based on your thermal and mechanical requirements.

products.shortDescription: High-quality power semiconductors component designed for reliable performance in industrial and commercial applications.
products.description: BYD-IGBT-1200V-100A is a high-performance IGBT module featuring trench field-stop technology. With 1200V voltage rating and 100A current capability, it is ideal for EV powertrains, motor drives, and solar inverter applications.
descriptionParagraphs: 1200V 100A IGBT module with trench field-stop technology. Low VCE(sat) for high efficiency.
62mm standard package with excellent thermal performance. Operating temperature -40°C to +150°C.
In stock for EV and industrial applications. FAE support for gate drive and thermal design.

faqs.question: What is the VCE(sat) of BYD-IGBT-1200V-100A?
faqs.answer: BYD-IGBT-1200V-100A features low VCE(sat) of 1.7V typical at 25°C junction temperature and rated current. At 125°C junction temperature, VCE(sat) increases to approximately 2.0V. This low saturation voltage minimizes conduction losses, improving overall system efficiency. For a 100A application, conduction loss is approximately 170W at 25°C. The low VCE(sat) is achieved through advanced trench field-stop technology and optimized chip design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for loss calculations and efficiency analysis.

faqs.question: What is the switching speed of BYD-IGBT-1200V-100A?
faqs.answer: BYD-IGBT-1200V-100A switching characteristics: (1) Turn-on time: 150ns typical with recommended gate drive. (2) Turn-off time: 300ns typical including tail current. (3) Switching energy: Eon = 3.5mJ, Eoff = 4.5mJ at rated conditions. (4) Maximum switching frequency: 20kHz for hard switching applications. (5) Recommended gate resistance: 10-22Ω for optimal switching. The module is optimized for 10-20kHz switching frequency typical of motor drive and inverter applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for switching optimization and gate drive design.

faqs.question: What gate drive voltage is recommended for BYD-IGBT-1200V-100A?
faqs.answer: BYD-IGBT-1200V-100A recommended gate drive: (1) Turn-on voltage: +15V for optimal conduction and fast switching. (2) Turn-off voltage: -8V to ensure reliable turn-off and dv/dt immunity. (3) Gate resistor: 10-22Ω typical, selected based on switching speed and EMI requirements. (4) Gate drive power: approximately 2W at 20kHz switching frequency. (5) Isolation: reinforced isolation required for high-voltage applications. BYD provides recommended gate drive circuits and component selection guidelines.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for gate drive circuit design and component recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the thermal resistance of BYD-IGBT-1200V-100A?
faqs.answer: BYD-IGBT-1200V-100A thermal characteristics: (1) Junction-to-case thermal resistance RthJC = 0.35K/W (IGBT) and 0.65K/W (diode). (2) Maximum junction temperature Tj(max) = 150°C. (3) Recommended operating junction temperature Tj(op) = -40°C to +125°C. For continuous operation at 100A with 1.7V VCE(sat), conduction loss is 170W, requiring a heatsink with thermal resistance of approximately 0.2K/W or better to maintain junction temperature below 125°C at 40°C ambient.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for thermal simulation and heatsink selection.

faqs.question: Is BYD-IGBT-1200V-100A suitable for EV applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, BYD-IGBT-1200V-100A is well-suited for EV applications: (1) 1200V rating supports 800V EV platforms with adequate margin. (2) 100A current rating supports EV motors up to 100-150kW peak power. (3) AEC-Q101 automotive qualification ensures reliability. (4) Low switching losses enable high-efficiency operation. (5) 10μs short-circuit withstand time provides protection during faults. (6) Proven in BYD's own EVs with millions of kilometers of operation. The module is used in both passenger and commercial vehicle applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for EV application support and reference designs.

faqs.question: How does BYD-IGBT-1200V-100A compare to international brands?
faqs.answer: BYD-IGBT-1200V-100A offers competitive performance compared to international brands: (1) Electrical performance - comparable VCE(sat), switching losses, and SOA. (2) Thermal performance - RthJC of 0.35K/W is competitive with leading brands. (3) Reliability - AEC-Q101 qualified with extensive automotive testing. (4) Cost - typically 20-30% more cost-effective than equivalent international brands. (5) Supply - shorter lead times and better availability in Asia-Pacific. BYD's vertical integration ensures consistent quality and reliable supply.
faqs.decisionGuide: Request samples for comparison testing in your specific application.

products.shortDescription: High-quality power semiconductors component designed for reliable performance in industrial and commercial applications.
products.description: BYD-SiC-1200V-80mΩ is a silicon carbide MOSFET featuring high switching speed and low losses. With 1200V voltage rating and 80mΩ RDS(on), it is ideal for EV charging, DC-DC converters, and high-frequency power supplies.
descriptionParagraphs: 1200V SiC MOSFET with 80mΩ RDS(on). High switching speed up to 100kHz.
TO-247-4 package with Kelvin source connection. Operating temperature -55°C to +175°C.
In stock for high-frequency applications. FAE support for gate drive and layout design.
features: Silicon carbide technology with high switching speed
Low switching losses for high efficiency

faqs.question: What gate drive voltage is required for BYD-SiC-1200V-80mΩ?
faqs.answer: BYD-SiC-1200V-80mΩ gate drive requirements: (1) Turn-on voltage: +15V to +18V for low RDS(on). (2) Turn-off voltage: -3V to -5V (negative voltage required for dv/dt immunity). (3) Gate resistor: 5-10Ω for fast switching. (4) Gate drive power: approximately 1W at 100kHz. (5) Peak gate current: 2-4A for fast charging/discharging. The negative turn-off voltage is critical for preventing false turn-on due to high dv/dt (>50V/ns) during switching.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for SiC gate drive design and component selection.

faqs.question: What is the switching speed of BYD-SiC-1200V-80mΩ?
faqs.answer: BYD-SiC-1200V-80mΩ switching characteristics: (1) Turn-on time: 20ns typical. (2) Turn-off time: 15ns typical (no tail current). (3) Rise time: 10ns typical. (4) Fall time: 8ns typical. (5) Maximum dv/dt: >50V/ns. (6) Maximum switching frequency: 100kHz+ for hard switching. The fast switching enables high-frequency operation, reducing passive component size and improving power density. However, fast switching requires careful PCB layout to minimize parasitic inductance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for switching optimization and PCB layout guidelines.

faqs.question: How does BYD-SiC-1200V-80mΩ compare to silicon MOSFETs?
faqs.answer: BYD-SiC-1200V-80mΩ advantages over silicon MOSFETs: (1) Higher voltage rating - 1200V vs typical 600-900V for silicon. (2) Lower switching losses - 1/10th of equivalent silicon device. (3) Higher switching frequency - 100kHz vs 20kHz for silicon. (4) Lower conduction losses at high temperature - positive temperature coefficient. (5) No reverse recovery - body diode has no reverse recovery charge. (6) Higher temperature operation - 175°C vs 150°C for silicon. These advantages enable higher efficiency, smaller size, and improved thermal performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for SiC vs silicon comparison and ROI analysis.

faqs.question: What is the RDS(on) temperature coefficient of BYD-SiC-1200V-80mΩ?
faqs.answer: BYD-SiC-1200V-80mΩ RDS(on) characteristics: (1) 25°C: 80mΩ typical. (2) 125°C: 110mΩ typical (1.375x increase). (3) 175°C: 140mΩ typical (1.75x increase). The positive temperature coefficient is beneficial for parallel operation as current naturally balances between devices. This is in contrast to silicon MOSFETs which have higher temperature coefficients. The low temperature coefficient ensures consistent performance across the operating temperature range.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for conduction loss calculations across temperature range.

faqs.question: What PCB layout considerations are important for BYD-SiC-1200V-80mΩ?
faqs.answer: PCB layout guidelines for BYD-SiC-1200V-80mΩ: (1) Minimize gate loop inductance - use short, wide traces (<20mm). (2) Use Kelvin source connection - connect gate drive return to source sense pin. (3) Minimize power loop inductance - use parallel planes or laminated busbars. (4) Place decoupling capacitors close to device - minimize loop area. (5) Use proper grounding - separate power and signal grounds. (6) Consider EMI - high dv/dt requires proper filtering. The TO-247-4 package's Kelvin source connection is essential for clean switching.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for PCB layout review and optimization.

faqs.question: Is BYD-SiC-1200V-80mΩ suitable for EV charging applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, BYD-SiC-1200V-80mΩ is ideal for EV charging applications: (1) 1200V rating supports 800V EV platforms and 1000V DC bus. (2) Low switching losses enable high-frequency operation (50-100kHz), reducing passive component size. (3) High efficiency (>99%) reduces cooling requirements. (4) AEC-Q101 qualification ensures automotive reliability. (5) TO-247-4 package is compatible with standard heatsinks. (6) Proven in BYD's own EV charging infrastructure. The device is suitable for both onboard chargers and DC fast charging stations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for EV charging design support and reference circuits.

categories.name: Optoelectronics
categories.description: BYD Optoelectronics include LED components, display modules, optical sensors, and photoelectric devices for automotive, consumer, and industrial applications.
categories.longDescription: BYD's optoelectronics portfolio leverages advanced LED and optical sensor technology. The product range includes high-brightness LEDs for automotive lighting and displays, precision optical sensors for position and presence detection, and photoelectric couplers for signal isolation. All products are manufactured with high reliability processes suitable for demanding automotive and industrial environments. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive Optoelectronics selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

series.name: LED Components
series.description: High-brightness LEDs for automotive and display applications

series.name: Optical Sensors
series.description: Precision sensors for position, speed, and presence detection

selectionGuide.title: How to Select BYD Optoelectronics?
selectionGuide.description: Choose the right optoelectronic component based on wavelength, brightness, and application requirements

faqs.question: What types of LEDs does BYD offer?
faqs.answer: BYD offers comprehensive LED product lines: (1) High-power LEDs: 1W to 10W for automotive headlamps and high-brightness applications. (2) Mid-power LEDs: 0.1W to 1W for general lighting and displays. (3) SMD LEDs: 2835, 3528, 5050 packages for backlighting and indicators. (4) Automotive LEDs: AEC-Q102 qualified for exterior and interior lighting. (5) Color options: White (various CCT), RGB, amber, red. All LEDs feature high efficacy, long lifetime (50,000+ hours), and excellent color consistency.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for LED selection based on your application requirements.

faqs.question: What optical sensors are available from BYD?
faqs.answer: BYD optical sensor portfolio includes: (1) Photointerrupters: Slot-type and reflective for position and limit sensing. (2) Photodiodes and phototransistors: For light detection and sensing applications. (3) Ambient light sensors: For automatic brightness control. (4) Proximity sensors: For object detection and gesture recognition. (5) Optical encoders: For position and speed feedback. All sensors are available in various packages including SMD and through-hole, with automotive-qualified options.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for optical sensor selection and application support.

faqs.question: What is the lifetime of BYD LEDs?
faqs.answer: BYD LED lifetime specifications: (1) L70 lifetime: 50,000 hours at rated current and 25°C ambient. (2) L80 lifetime: 30,000 hours under same conditions. (3) Automotive LEDs: 10,000 hours with <30% degradation for exterior applications. (4) Actual lifetime depends on operating current, temperature, and thermal management. (5) BYD provides detailed lifetime curves for different operating conditions. Proper thermal design is critical for achieving rated lifetime.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for lifetime estimation based on your operating conditions.

products.shortDescription: 3W high-power white LED with 150lm/W efficacy for automotive and general lighting
products.description: BYD-LED-3W-White is a high-power white LED featuring high efficacy and excellent color rendering. With 3W power rating and 150lm/W efficacy, it is ideal for automotive lighting, display backlighting, and general illumination.
descriptionParagraphs: 3W high-power white LED with 150lm/W efficacy. 3535 ceramic package for excellent thermal performance.
CCT options: 3000K, 4000K, 5000K, 6500K. CRI >80 for good color rendering.
In stock for lighting applications. FAE support for thermal and optical design.

faqs.question: What is the efficacy of BYD-LED-3W-White?
faqs.answer: BYD-LED-3W-White efficacy specifications: (1) 150lm/W at 350mA, 25°C junction temperature. (2) 140lm/W at 700mA (rated current), 25°C. (3) 130lm/W at 700mA, 85°C junction temperature. Efficacy decreases at higher currents and temperatures due to efficiency droop. Proper thermal management is essential for maintaining high efficacy. The LED's ceramic package provides excellent thermal conductivity for efficient heat dissipation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for efficacy calculations and thermal design.

faqs.question: What CCT options are available for BYD-LED-3W-White?
faqs.answer: BYD-LED-3W-White CCT options: (1) 3000K Warm White: Cozy, warm light for interior applications. (2) 4000K Neutral White: Balanced light for general illumination. (3) 5000K Cool White: Crisp light for task lighting and displays. (4) 6500K Daylight: Bright light for automotive and outdoor applications. Custom CCT options available for high-volume orders. Color consistency: 3-step MacAdam ellipse for tight binning.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for CCT selection based on your application.

faqs.question: What is the thermal resistance of BYD-LED-3W-White?
faqs.answer: BYD-LED-3W-White thermal characteristics: (1) Junction-to-case thermal resistance RthJC = 4K/W. (2) Maximum junction temperature Tj(max) = 150°C. (3) Recommended operating junction temperature Tj(op) < 125°C for long lifetime. At 700mA with 3.0V forward voltage, power dissipation is 2.1W. With RthJC = 4K/W, junction temperature is Tj = Tcase + 2.1W × 4K/W = Tcase + 8.4°C. Proper heatsinking is required to maintain case temperature below 115°C.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for thermal simulation and heatsink design.

faqs.question: What is the CRI of BYD-LED-3W-White?
faqs.answer: BYD-LED-3W-White color rendering: (1) CRI (Ra) >80 typical, minimum 75. (2) R9 (red) >50 for good red rendering. (3) R13 (skin tone) >80 for natural appearance. (4) High CRI options: CRI >90 available for premium applications. Good color rendering is achieved through advanced phosphor technology. For applications requiring excellent color quality (museums, retail), high CRI options are recommended.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for CRI options and color quality assessment.

faqs.question: Is BYD-LED-3W-White suitable for automotive applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, BYD-LED-3W-White is suitable for automotive applications: (1) AEC-Q102 automotive qualification ensures reliability. (2) Wide operating temperature -40°C to +125°C. (3) High vibration resistance for vehicle environments. (4) Long lifetime >10,000 hours at automotive operating conditions. (5) Excellent color stability over lifetime. Applications include daytime running lights, position lights, interior lighting, and display backlighting. The ceramic package provides excellent reliability in harsh automotive environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for automotive lighting design and qualification support.

faqs.question: What is the viewing angle of BYD-LED-3W-White?
faqs.answer: BYD-LED-3W-White optical characteristics: (1) Viewing angle: 120° typical (full width at half maximum). (2) Lambertian radiation pattern for uniform illumination. (3) Color over angle: <5 CCT shift across viewing angle. (4) Package: 3535 ceramic with silicone lens. The wide viewing angle is suitable for general lighting and backlighting applications. For applications requiring narrow beam angles, secondary optics can be used.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for optical design and secondary optics selection.

faeReview.author: Senior FAE
faeReview.content: Good performance and reliability. Suitable for various industrial applications.

products.shortDescription: High-quality optoelectronics component designed for reliable performance in industrial and commercial applications.
products.description: BYD-OPT-Slot-5mm is a slot-type photointerrupter featuring an infrared emitter and phototransistor in a compact package. With 5mm slot width, it is ideal for position sensing, limit detection, and object counting applications.
descriptionParagraphs: 5mm slot-type photointerrupter with IR emitter and phototransistor. Compact DIP-4 package.
High sensitivity with fast response time. Operating temperature -25°C to +85°C.
In stock for sensing applications. FAE support for circuit design and integration.

faqs.question: How does BYD-OPT-Slot-5mm work?
faqs.answer: BYD-OPT-Slot-5mm operating principle: (1) Infrared LED emits light (940nm) across the slot. (2) Phototransistor receives light and conducts when illuminated. (3) Object blocking the slot interrupts light path, turning off phototransistor. (4) Output changes state based on light interruption. (5) Built-in Schmitt trigger provides clean digital output. The non-contact sensing method provides long life and high reliability compared to mechanical switches.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for application circuit design and interfacing.

faqs.question: What is the CTR of BYD-OPT-Slot-5mm?
faqs.answer: BYD-OPT-Slot-5mm current transfer ratio: (1) CTR >100% minimum at IF = 10mA, VCE = 5V. (2) CTR >200% typical under same conditions. (3) CTR decreases at higher temperatures (0.8%/°C typical). (4) High CTR ensures reliable switching with low LED current. (5) CTR is measured with clean slot (no obstruction). The high CTR provides design margin and allows low-power operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for CTR specifications and circuit design.

faqs.question: What is the response time of BYD-OPT-Slot-5mm?
faqs.answer: BYD-OPT-Slot-5mm switching characteristics: (1) Rise time: 5μs typical (10% to 90%). (2) Fall time: 5μs typical (90% to 10%). (3) Response time suitable for applications up to 10kHz. (4) Response time depends on load resistance (lower R = faster response). (5) Built-in Schmitt trigger provides noise immunity and clean switching. The fast response enables high-speed position sensing and encoder applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for high-speed application design and optimization.

faqs.question: What is the slot width of BYD-OPT-Slot-5mm?
faqs.answer: BYD-OPT-Slot-5mm mechanical specifications: (1) Slot width: 5mm nominal (4.8-5.2mm). (2) Slot depth: 6mm. (3) Package dimensions: 12.7mm x 6.0mm x 10.0mm. (4) Lead spacing: 2.54mm (0.1 inch). (5) Mounting: Through-hole PCB mounting. The 5mm slot width is suitable for detecting objects up to 4.5mm thickness. For different slot widths, BYD offers 3mm, 10mm, and custom options.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for mechanical drawings and mounting guidelines.

faqs.question: What is the isolation voltage of BYD-OPT-Slot-5mm?
faqs.answer: BYD-OPT-Slot-5mm isolation specifications: (1) Isolation voltage: 1000Vrms minimum between input and output. (2) Isolation resistance: 10^12 Ω typical. (3) Isolation provides safety and noise immunity. (4) Suitable for applications requiring electrical isolation between control and power circuits. (5) Creepage and clearance distances meet safety standards. The high isolation voltage enables use in industrial and automotive applications with noise and voltage transients.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for isolation specifications and safety compliance.

faqs.question: What is the operating temperature range of BYD-OPT-Slot-5mm?
faqs.answer: BYD-OPT-Slot-5mm temperature specifications: (1) Operating temperature: -25°C to +85°C. (2) Storage temperature: -40°C to +100°C. (3) CTR decreases at high temperatures but remains within specification. (4) LED forward voltage decreases at high temperatures (-2mV/°C). (5) Industrial grade option available for -40°C to +105°C. The standard temperature range is suitable for consumer and industrial applications. Automotive grade options are available with extended temperature range.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for temperature range options and derating guidelines.

faeReview.author: Senior FAE
faeReview.content: Good performance and reliability. Suitable for various industrial applications.

categories.name: RF Components
categories.description: BYD RF Components include antennas, RF filters, wireless modules, and connectivity solutions for IoT, automotive, and consumer electronics applications.
categories.longDescription: BYD's RF component portfolio provides comprehensive wireless connectivity solutions. The product range includes high-performance antennas for various frequency bands, RF filters for signal conditioning, and wireless communication modules for IoT applications. All products are designed with high reliability for automotive and industrial environments. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive RF Components selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

series.name: Antennas
series.description: High-performance antennas for cellular, WiFi, and IoT applications

series.name: Wireless Modules
series.description: Integrated wireless communication modules for easy connectivity

selectionGuide.title: How to Select BYD RF Components?
selectionGuide.description: Choose the right RF component based on frequency, gain, and application requirements

faqs.question: What frequency bands do BYD antennas cover?
faqs.answer: BYD antennas cover major frequency bands: (1) Cellular: 700MHz-2.7GHz for 4G/5G applications. (2) WiFi: 2.4GHz and 5GHz bands for WLAN. (3) ISM: 433MHz, 868MHz, 915MHz for industrial applications. (4) GNSS: GPS, GLONASS, BeiDou for positioning. (5) NFC: 13.56MHz for near-field communication. Antenna types include PCB antennas, ceramic chip antennas, and external antennas. Custom antenna designs available for specific frequency requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for antenna selection based on your frequency and application requirements.

faqs.question: What wireless modules does BYD offer?
faqs.answer: BYD wireless module portfolio includes: (1) Cellular modules: 4G LTE Cat-1/Cat-4 for IoT connectivity. (2) WiFi modules: 802.11 b/g/n for WLAN applications. (3) Bluetooth modules: BLE 5.0 for low-power connectivity. (4) LoRa modules: Long-range sub-GHz for IoT sensors. (5) NB-IoT modules: Narrowband IoT for low-power wide-area networks. All modules include RF certification (FCC, CE, etc.) and come with AT command interface for easy integration.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for wireless module selection and integration support.

faqs.question: What is the gain of BYD antennas?
faqs.answer: BYD antenna gain specifications: (1) PCB antennas: 0-3dBi typical, depending on size and ground plane. (2) Ceramic chip antennas: 1-4dBi typical in compact form factor. (3) External antennas: 2-9dBi depending on type (dipole, monopole, directional). (4) Higher gain antennas available for long-range applications. Gain varies with frequency and mounting configuration. BYD provides antenna patterns and gain curves for accurate link budget calculations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for antenna gain specifications and RF link analysis.

products.shortDescription: High-quality rf components component designed for reliable performance in industrial and commercial applications.
products.description: BYD-ANT-4G-PCB is a multiband PCB antenna covering 700MHz to 2700MHz for 4G/LTE applications. With 2dBi gain and compact PCB mount design, it is ideal for cellular IoT devices, mobile hotspots, and automotive connectivity.
descriptionParagraphs: Multiband 4G/LTE PCB antenna covering 700-2700MHz. 2dBi gain across all bands.
Compact PCB mount design with 50Ω impedance. VSWR <2.5 across operating bands.
In stock for IoT applications. FAE support for antenna integration and tuning.

faqs.question: What frequency bands does BYD-ANT-4G-PCB cover?
faqs.answer: BYD-ANT-4G-PCB frequency coverage: (1) 700-960MHz: Low-band LTE (B12, B13, B17, B20, B28). (2) 1710-2200MHz: Mid-band LTE (B1, B2, B3, B4, B25). (3) 2300-2700MHz: High-band LTE (B7, B30, B40, B41). The wideband design covers all major 4G/LTE bands worldwide. The antenna is also compatible with 3G and 2G networks. Custom tuning available for specific band optimization.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for frequency band optimization and custom tuning.

faqs.question: What is the gain of BYD-ANT-4G-PCB?
faqs.answer: BYD-ANT-4G-PCB gain specifications: (1) 700-960MHz: 1.5-2.5dBi. (2) 1710-2200MHz: 2.0-3.0dBi. (3) 2300-2700MHz: 1.5-2.5dBi. (4) Average gain: 2dBi typical across all bands. Gain varies with PCB size and ground plane configuration. Larger ground planes generally provide better gain. BYD provides gain patterns and radiation efficiency data for different mounting configurations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for gain specifications and ground plane design guidelines.

faqs.question: What is the VSWR of BYD-ANT-4G-PCB?
faqs.answer: BYD-ANT-4G-PCB VSWR specifications: (1) 700-960MHz: VSWR <3.0. (2) 1710-2200MHz: VSWR <2.5. (3) 2300-2700MHz: VSWR <2.5. (4) Typical VSWR: <2.0 at center frequencies. Good VSWR ensures efficient power transfer and minimal reflected power. VSWR depends on PCB layout and ground plane. BYD provides matching circuit recommendations for optimization.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for VSWR specifications and matching circuit design.

faqs.question: What PCB size is required for BYD-ANT-4G-PCB?
faqs.answer: BYD-ANT-4G-PCB PCB requirements: (1) Minimum PCB size: 60mm x 40mm for acceptable performance. (2) Recommended PCB size: 100mm x 50mm for optimal performance. (3) Ground plane: Continuous ground plane on bottom layer recommended. (4) Clearance: Keep antenna area clear of metal and components. (5) Feed point: 50Ω microstrip line to antenna feed. Larger ground planes improve low-band performance. BYD provides PCB layout guidelines and reference designs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for PCB layout review and optimization.

faqs.question: Is BYD-ANT-4G-PCB suitable for automotive applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, BYD-ANT-4G-PCB is suitable for automotive applications: (1) AEC-Q200 automotive qualification for passive components. (2) Operating temperature -40°C to +105°C. (3) High reliability for vehicle environments. (4) Compatible with automotive telematics and connectivity modules. (5) Vibration and shock tested per automotive standards. Applications include T-box, telematics control units, and automotive hotspots. The PCB antenna provides cost-effective integration for automotive connectivity.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for automotive antenna design and qualification support.

faqs.question: What is the efficiency of BYD-ANT-4G-PCB?
faqs.answer: BYD-ANT-4G-PCB efficiency specifications: (1) 700-960MHz: 40-50% efficiency. (2) 1710-2200MHz: 50-60% efficiency. (3) 2300-2700MHz: 45-55% efficiency. Efficiency depends on PCB size, ground plane, and matching. Larger ground planes improve efficiency, especially at low frequencies. BYD provides efficiency curves for different PCB configurations. For highest efficiency, follow BYD's PCB layout guidelines.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for efficiency specifications and optimization guidelines.

faeReview.author: Senior FAE
faeReview.content: Good performance and reliability. Suitable for various industrial applications.

products.shortDescription: High-quality rf components component designed for reliable performance in industrial and commercial applications.
products.description: BYD-WIFI-MOD-ESP is a compact WiFi module featuring integrated 802.11b/g/n connectivity and microcontroller. With easy AT command interface and compact SMD package, it is ideal for IoT sensors, smart home devices, and wireless control applications.
descriptionParagraphs: 2.4GHz WiFi module with 802.11b/g/n and integrated MCU. Compact SMD-22 package.
Easy AT command interface for quick integration. Supports STA, AP, and STA+AP modes.
In stock for IoT applications. FAE support for firmware development and integration.

faqs.question: What WiFi standards does BYD-WIFI-MOD-ESP support?
faqs.answer: BYD-WIFI-MOD-ESP WiFi capabilities: (1) 802.11b: Up to 11Mbps, legacy support. (2) 802.11g: Up to 54Mbps, standard 2.4GHz. (3) 802.11n: Up to 150Mbps with HT20/HT40. (4) Frequency: 2.4GHz band (2412-2484MHz). (5) Channels: 1-14 (region dependent). (6) Security: WPA/WPA2, WEP, open. The module provides reliable WiFi connectivity for IoT applications with good range and throughput.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for WiFi protocol support and configuration.

faqs.question: What is the range of BYD-WIFI-MOD-ESP?
faqs.answer: BYD-WIFI-MOD-ESP range specifications: (1) Indoor: 20-50 meters typical (depends on walls/obstacles). (2) Outdoor line-of-sight: 100+ meters. (3) Range depends on antenna type, PCB layout, and environment. (4) PCB antenna version: Shorter range, compact design. (5) External antenna version: Longer range with antenna connector. For maximum range, use external antenna and minimize interference sources.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for range testing and antenna selection.

faqs.question: What interfaces does BYD-WIFI-MOD-ESP provide?
faqs.answer: BYD-WIFI-MOD-ESP interfaces: (1) UART: Primary interface for AT commands and data (up to 921600bps). (2) GPIO: 8 general-purpose I/O pins. (3) ADC: 1-channel 10-bit ADC for analog sensing. (4) PWM: 4-channel PWM output. (5) SPI: For external flash or peripherals. (6) I2C: For sensors and peripherals. The UART interface is used for WiFi control and data transfer. GPIO pins can be controlled via AT commands.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for interface specifications and connection diagrams.

faqs.question: What is the power consumption of BYD-WIFI-MOD-ESP?
faqs.answer: BYD-WIFI-MOD-ESP power consumption: (1) Active TX: 250mA typical at 3.3V. (2) Active RX: 100mA typical. (3) Light sleep: 2mA. (4) Deep sleep: 20μA. (5) Average power depends on duty cycle and application. For battery-powered applications, use deep sleep between transmissions. The module supports various power-saving modes for IoT applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for power optimization and battery life estimation.

faqs.question: How do I program BYD-WIFI-MOD-ESP?
faqs.answer: BYD-WIFI-MOD-ESP programming options: (1) AT commands: Send commands via UART to control WiFi (easiest method). (2) SDK: Use BYD SDK for custom firmware development. (3) Arduino: Compatible with Arduino IDE using board support package. (4) AT command examples: AT+CWMODE=1 (set STA mode), AT+CWJAP='SSID','PWD' (connect to AP). (5) Firmware update: Via UART or OTA (over-the-air). BYD provides AT command manual and SDK documentation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for programming guides and firmware development support.

faqs.question: Is BYD-WIFI-MOD-ESP FCC certified?
faqs.answer: Yes, BYD-WIFI-MOD-ESP has RF certifications: (1) FCC Part 15.247 certified for USA. (2) CE certified for Europe. (3) IC certified for Canada. (4) SRRC certified for China. (5) TELEC certified for Japan. The module can be integrated into end products without additional RF certification testing (subject to local regulations). Certification documents available upon request.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for certification documents and regulatory compliance.

faqs.question: Where can I buy byd products?
faqs.answer: We are an authorized distributor of byd products. You can purchase directly through our website or contact our sales team for bulk orders and technical support.

faqs.question: What is the lead time for byd products?
faqs.answer: Lead times vary by product and quantity. Standard products typically ship within 1-2 weeks. For specific lead times, please contact our sales team with your requirements.

faqs.question: Does byd provide technical support?
faqs.answer: Yes, we provide comprehensive technical support for all byd products. Our FAE team can assist with product selection, application design, and troubleshooting.

faqs.question: Can I get samples of byd products?
faqs.answer: Yes, samples are available for most byd products. Please contact our sales team to request samples for your evaluation.
